title: Connect OAuth UI to post-auth cart merge
verify: Login page exposes Google and VK ID with pending, cancel, safe failure, and retry states.
Completion retrieves session customer and invokes CartProvider.mergeAfterAuthentication when a guest reference exists.
Merge success/no-source reaches authenticated_ready; recoverable failure preserves session/source and blocks continuation with retry.
Completion URL and UI never expose provider code, state, token, raw error, or customer PII.

verify.reason: Independent T3 verification found fail-open merge-result mapping and missing component concurrency/stale-result coverage.
T3 re-verification found real null+idle no-source mismatch and incomplete malformed handoff validation.
T3 functional and semantic verification passed after final bounded retry 2/2.

evidence_required: URL/error/privacy assertions and storefront build output.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Use the existing CartProvider mergeAfterAuthentication handoff without changing backend merge semantics.
Keep authentication valid on recoverable merge failure while blocking checkout continuation.
Render only sanitized provider and merge errors.
invariants: Guest source reference changes only after validated backend merge success.
Provider callback query/token data never reaches rendered UI.
Only authenticated_ready may consume the return path.
purpose: Deliver buyer-visible Google/VK login completion and preserve cart continuity through the existing secure merge boundary.
success_outcome: Both login choices establish customer state and converge on merged/no-source readiness, with recoverable merge failure preventing silent checkout continuation.
anti_goals: Do not change FT-003 backend merge behavior.
Do not add checkout fields, orders, or payments.
Do not display provider payloads or customer PII.

stop_conditions: Existing FT-003 handoff cannot represent success/no-source/recoverable failure.
Completion requires exposing provider query or token data to UI.
Auth state cannot remain truthful during merge retry.
